label: Compensation Basics
title: Compensation Basics Quiz
question: What does a compa-ratio of 0.85 indicate about an employee's pay relative to the salary range midpoint?

options.label: The employee is paid 15% above midpoint
The employee is paid 15% below midpoint
The employee is paid exactly at midpoint
The employee's pay cannot be determined

label: Total Rewards
title: Total Rewards Foundations
question: Which statement best describes Total Rewards?

options.label: A payroll process focused only on salary payment dates
The full combination of financial and non-financial value employees receive from work
A benefits-only program managed by external vendors
A performance rating form used during annual reviews

label: Compensation
title: Variable Pay
question: Which item is an example of variable pay?

options.label: Fixed monthly salary
A performance bonus linked to business results
Annual leave entitlement
Health insurance coverage

label: Salary Structures
title: Salary Ranges
question: What does the salary midpoint usually represent?

options.label: The minimum salary an employee can ever earn
The target market or fully competent pay point for the grade
The total company payroll budget
The maximum bonus opportunity

label: Benefits
title: Benefits Basics
question: What best describes employee benefits?

options.label: Indirect rewards that support employee security, wellbeing or quality of life
Only salary increases paid once a year
Only job evaluation scores
Only sales incentive formulas

label: Pay Equity
title: Pay Equity
question: What does pay equity mean?

options.label: Everyone must always earn exactly the same amount
Pay differences should be fair, explainable and based on legitimate factors
Employees should not know anything about pay
Only executives need pay review

label: Job Evaluation
title: Job Evaluation
question: What does job evaluation assess?

options.label: The relative value of a job, not the personal worth of the employee
Only an employee's performance rating
Only the employee's length of service
Only the market salary survey title

label: Compensation
title: Pay Compression
question: What is pay compression?

options.label: When all benefits are paid in cash
When pay differences between new and experienced employees become too small
When salary grades are too wide to manage
When payroll is processed earlier than usual

label: Salary Structures
title: Range Spread
question: What does range spread measure?

options.label: The number of employees in a salary grade
The width of the salary range from minimum to maximum
The number of job descriptions created
The distance between two departments

label: Total Rewards
title: Employee Value Proposition
question: What is the Employee Value Proposition most closely connected to?

options.label: The organization's complete promise of value to employees
The payroll tax schedule for each employee
The company's office seating arrangement
The legal name of the employer only

label: Market Pricing
title: Market Pricing
question: What is market pricing?

options.label: Comparing jobs to external pay data for similar work
Setting all salaries based on employee preference
Replacing job evaluation with job titles only
Calculating only payroll taxes

label: Benefits
title: Benefit Utilization
question: What is benefit utilization?

options.label: How much or how often eligible employees use a benefit
The salary midpoint for a pay grade
The number of open jobs in a department
The annual performance rating scale

label: Compensation
title: Total Cash
question: What is total cash compensation?

options.label: Base salary plus cash incentives or bonuses
Only non-cash benefits
Only retirement contributions
Only employer-provided learning programs

label: Compensation Governance
title: Pay Governance
question: What is one purpose of compensation governance?

options.label: To create consistent rules for offers, increases, promotions and exceptions
To remove all manager involvement in pay discussions
To prevent HR from using salary data
To make pay decisions slower without reason

label: Salary Structures
title: Range Penetration
question: What is range penetration used to understand?

options.label: How far an employee's pay has moved through the salary range
How many benefits an employee selected
How many candidates applied for a role
How long a course takes to complete

label: Communication
title: Rewards Communication
question: Why is communication important in Total Rewards?

options.label: It helps employees understand the value and purpose of reward programs
It guarantees every employee will like every benefit
It eliminates the need for governance
It replaces market benchmarking

label: Benefits
title: Wellbeing
question: Which program is commonly linked to employee wellbeing?

options.label: Salary grade midpoint
Employee assistance or mental health support
Job evaluation committee score
Market pricing survey

label: Rewards Analyst
title: Analyst Skills
question: Which skill is especially important for a rewards analyst?

options.label: Data analysis and spreadsheet confidence
Avoiding numbers completely
Ignoring employee questions
Using job titles as the only evidence

label: Salary Structures
title: Salary Grades
question: Which statement best describes a salary grade?

options.label: A grouping of roles with similar internal value or level
A list of employee benefit vendors
A payroll tax category only
A manager's personal pay preference

label: Compensation
title: Internal Equity
question: Which situation could create internal equity concerns?

options.label: Two similar roles are placed in the same grade after evaluation
A new hire is paid much more than experienced peers without a clear reason
Salary ranges are reviewed using market data
Managers receive pay communication training

label: Benefits
title: Retirement Benefits
question: Which benefit most directly supports long-term financial security?

options.label: Office parking allocation
Retirement or pension plan
Job title change
Performance appraisal form

label: Total Rewards
title: Balanced Approach
question: Which option best reflects a balanced Total Rewards approach?

options.label: Designing pay, benefits, wellbeing, recognition and growth around employee and business needs
Increasing salaries while removing all support programs
Copying competitor benefits without checking workforce needs
Using job titles alone to decide all rewards

label: Compensation
title: Compensation Definition
question: What best describes compensation?

options.label: All forms of direct financial reward provided for work performed
Only employee wellness programs
Only company culture initiatives
Only unpaid recognition messages

label: Salary Structures
title: Below Range Minimum
question: An employee paid below the range minimum should usually be:

options.label: Automatically promoted without review
Ignored until the next job evaluation cycle
Moved to a different benefit plan
Reviewed for possible adjustment, data accuracy and role fit

label: Pay Equity
title: Legitimate Pay Factors
question: Which factor may legitimately explain pay differences?

options.label: Manager favoritism
Relevant experience, skills, performance or role scope
Personal negotiation pressure only
Unrecorded historical habit

label: Benefits
title: Enrollment Communication
question: What is a practical goal of benefits enrollment communication?

options.label: To help employees make informed choices before deadlines
To discourage employees from using benefits
To hide eligibility rules
To replace all HR policies

label: Compensation
title: Base vs Variable
question: Which statement is accurate?

options.label: Base pay is fixed compensation, while incentives are usually variable
Base pay is always optional
Incentives should never have measures
Compensation excludes cash payments

label: Total Rewards
title: Non-Cash Rewards
question: Which item is usually considered a non-cash reward?

options.label: Annual base salary
Career development opportunity
Sales commission
Overtime payment

label: Total Rewards
title: Strategy Alignment
question: Why should Total Rewards align with business strategy?

options.label: So reward investments support attraction, retention, performance and workforce priorities
So HR can avoid using data in reward decisions
So all employees receive identical rewards regardless of role
So benefits can replace salary structures

label: Benefits
title: Benefits Strategy
question: What should a benefits strategy balance?

options.label: Employee needs, market practice, affordability and business priorities
Employee preferences only
Vendor preferences only
The number of pages in a policy document
